title: oxybaphus coahuilensis
intro: Oxybaphus coahuilensis, also known as the Coahuila Four O'clock, is a flowering plant species native to North America, particularly in the southwestern regions of the United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Oxybaphus coahuilensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable resource in natural remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
